title: The Jackdaw and the Peacocks
lines: A Jackdaw found some bright feathers from a Peacock and stuck them on his own body.
He strutted about among the Peacocks, hoping to deceive them.
But they soon saw through his disguise and plucked out the feathers, pecking at him until he fled.
He returned to his own kind, but they no longer recognized him.
'You are a fool," they said, 'to leave us for strangers who would harm you.'
moral: Trying to be something you are not leads only to humiliation.
